review_text: To quote AURORA: "Feel the rage!"
Great movie! One of the best! A very powerful Miyazaki work

Miyzaki’s magnum opus. What originally was to be his last movie, also ends up being the finest work of his career. The greatest crescendo of any filmmaker. Fortunately, he decided to continue working and gave us a couple more masterpieces before his end (supposedly). But this is his most engaging, compelling and epic film, along with being the single greatest anime feature of all time.
Miyazaki masterpiece

A classic Miyazaki film that takes place in feudal (Presumably Muromachi Period) Japan. Filled with samurai, spirits and adventure, this movie will take you on a quest that you will never forget. Follow the adventure of a young boy who must go on an adventure to stop evil from spreading across the world. With guest voice appearances from Gillian Anderson and Billy Bob Thornton you are sure to love this classic!
This is one of the greatest movies ever made. Deep, spiritual, moving, and confrontational. Art is meant to elicit deep emotions and this movie does not fall short of being great art.
Beautiful Music & Animation. (English Sub)-(9/10) (English Dub)-(10/10)
First time seeing it and in IMAX made it 10x better
All time classic meant to be seen in theaters

One of the most valuable additions to Studio Ghibli's collection. Its style and story are as hard as rock candy, and it serves as an absorbing parable about the importance of nature over man and the duty to protect it. A must-see.

Definitely the most violent of the Studio Ghibli films, and tied for my favorite of them. Absolutely stunning. Amazing story as usual, and fantastic English dub. While I love the original Japanese VA, listening to Claire Danes' voice as Princess Mononoke (San) is like throwing on a soft blanket. Backed up by other amazing VAs like Minnie Driver, Billy Thornton, and John DiMaggio, it might be one of the best English dubs out there.

The film is incredibly imaginative and the animation is beautiful. The score is wonderful, and the story has both scope and individual moments, creating a really fantastical world. It's a little weird! In a good way. The message of the film, offered through metaphors, is very meaningful. Hayao Miyazaki delivers a lot of depth and connection, even to what's still happening in the world nearly 30 years later. And the characters are rich, complex, and imperfect. Human and nuanced.
A second masterpiece to Miyazaki film but Spirit away will always be number one masterpiece
This was one of my favorite movies I ever watch ever since I was a kid and me being older I love this movie more now after rewatch also when I was a kid watch this movie like 23 times as a kid man.

"The movie was highly praised, but I honestly couldn’t understand why. I found it incredibly boring, the storyline didn’t appeal to me, and I couldn’t take away any meaningful message from it. I just didn’t get the point.
One of my all time favorite movies!!! Finally got to see it on the big screen!

A very entertaining and beautifully crafted film from Hayao Miyazaki. The animation is wonderful and gave me a warm sense of nostalgia—even though it was my first time watching it, it reminded me of other great animated films. The story is engaging, the characters are fun, and some of the gory moments are surprisingly stylish and satisfying to watch. Honestly, it’s probably the best £7.50 I’ve ever spent, especially with the bonus of seeing it on IMAX for my first viewing. Definitely recommended!
It was my first time watching this in theater and it was beautiful

I thought Spirited Away was Hayao Miyazaki best work but after seeing this.. I’m not sure what to think anymore. I wish I had grown up on this like I did like I did other Studio Ghibli movies. Although, I see maybe why as a kid I wasn’t shown this. Seeing a beheading in a Miyazaki movie was crazy.

I was disappointed Danny Devito didn’t pop out of one of those trees that fell in IMAX, however no notes besides that, and it was excellent. I also really appreciated the nods to/cameos of the South Carolina fires, as they got the color right and everything for the smoke I’ve been seeing lately! There is also a “run Forrest, RUN” moment when Ashitaka is running in a literal forest that was riveting!
It's breathtaking in every way.

It's an incredibly perfect animation. I got goosebumps when the OST started playing. Hayao Miyazawa is a genius. If this movie came out in the 21st century, it would definitely be the best 21st century movie in existence.
One of the best. Amazing looking film, which is sadly still relevant with its message about how we treat the planet.

Not enough can be said for one of the greatest films of all time...not just one of the greatest Anime's of all time. Even better than Spirited Away! It's a fun, thrilling, and emotionally extraodinary film that's parallel to the likes of Titanic and Lion King. The music gives you goosebumps; the writing makes you feel things you just do not feel but once in a blue moon. Although there's violence which you may find unusual for animation...please understand it's tasteful and dignified for the story. For a once in a lifetime experience, open your mind to Princess Mononoke.

Directed by Hayao Miyazaki, who directed both versions!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! The voice cast pronounces the script in either Japanese or English words!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Rating: 87%. This was a beautiful film. The animation was very good for its time. The sound, music, and English voice acting were all solid. The plot itself was very entertaining and well done. Like most Ghibli movies they did a good job of coming up with interesting and creative charcters. There were some aspects of the plot I didn't enjoy as much, but still good overall.
The best of the best studio ghibli hands down
I loved it! I hope they do more in IMAX!
4.5/10 — "Pretty Bad" -(Seen on 5/10/25)

All Time Classic. The story reminds me off a Final Fantasy game plot almost. It's basically an epic film with war, love, and involves forces beyond our understanding. The english voice acting is really good all around. Everything else is masterful. The animation is gorgous, the soundtrack is sweeping, beautiful, peaceful and memorable. It masterfully weaves the forest/curse of our main hero with the war between the two factions effortlessly. On top of that there is just alway something visually amazing to look at and is super distinct, haunting, beautiful, and always something we've never seen before. The biggest thing to is this 100% has an enviormentalist message to it. But it never feels on the nose or putting the message first before good storytelling and great characters to tell it through. This is how you do it. Everyone should give this a try once.

影片调度挥斥方遒，阿席达卡的动线构成了巧妙的串联，角色的立场与性格在每一幕叙述中愈发清晰；对炼铁、运输、狩猎等生产生活场景的细致刻画让宏大的主题有了坚实的影像基础，影院观赏的视听享受更是让本作历久弥新、引人入胜。原始信仰褪去、火器一枪弑神，山兽神自身也只能退化为灵怪，山火般掠过万物、以“毁灭—重建”的方式暂缓对峙。复杂的多重矛盾在故事中缠绕交织，“各自安好”式的结尾则袒露了宫崎骏的无奈：无论兽神何等漫山遍野、无论人类如何内斗互害，“向自然要发展”的人类社会都会在历史洪流中前行下去。如今回顾确有雾里看花之感，但其中包含的悲剧性依旧如侵蚀阿席达卡躯体的诅咒，具体实在、发人深省。

Princess Mononoke is peak Studio Ghibli—a hauntingly beautiful blend of myth, nature, and raw emotion. Ashitaka’s cursed journey into a world at war with itself reveals that neither side—man nor beast—is truly evil. The animation is stunning, the themes are timeless, and the characters live in moral gray areas that keep you thinking long after it ends. It’s not just a movie—it’s a meditation on coexistence, wrapped in gorgeous, hand-drawn chaos.
Original format made seeing this treasure an experience. IMAX experience worth seeing this majestic endeavor again, and again.
Seeing it on the big screen makes it so much more enjoyable!

Review from Japan. I love this movie. And I especially like the scene where the leper talks to Ashitaka, but I think the English subtitles are incorrect. The following is a machine-translated text of the scene from Japanese. ----- Young man, I understand your anger and sorrow, for I too have been cursed. I understand, but please do not kill the lady. She is the only one who treated us as human beings. Do not fear our sickness, wash and bandage my rotten flesh... "Life is truly painful and hard. I curse the world, I curse others, and yet I want to live." Forgive her for my foolishness. ---- However, the English subtitles translate the double-quote as follows ---- Life is suffering. It is hard. The world is cursed. But still, you find reasons to keep living. ---- The meaning of “I want to live even if it hard so much that I curse the world.” and “You must live even if the world is cursed” are completely different. Of course, there must be some kind of intention of the translator, but I think this translation is inappropriate.

Flawless film from one of the world's greatest studios. Beautifully made, sophisticated and incredibly deep storytelling. A timely reminder of the failure of western film making, terminally determined to cram crassly written and thought out messaging down a shrinking audiences throat. We can be thankful to streaming, which has opened up a life times worth of noble tales, dubbed in many languages, so Follywood can be ignored.
